note: CTools is a developer utility suite with no admin configuration screen (`configure: null`); it is consumed entirely through services and plugins. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded) and probe load-bearing, ctools-specific facts an agent would otherwise have to read source to answer.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want to turn several forms into one multi-step flow using CTools. Which base class do I subclass, and which method defines the ordered list of steps?
evals.expected_output: Subclass \Drupal\ctools\Wizard\FormWizardBase (implements FormWizardInterface) and implement getOperations($cached_values) to return the ordered steps, each ['title' => ..., 'form' => SomeForm::class].

evals.prompt: In a CTools form wizard, how do the individual step forms read and write the data collected so far across steps? Show the call.
evals.expected_output: Step forms read/write shared state via $form_state->getTemporaryValue('wizard'); the WizardFactory stores per-step values in a SerializableTempstore and clears it on finish.

evals.prompt: I want to derive a related context (e.g. a node's author user) from a base context in CTools. What plugin type do I use, what namespace/discovery directory holds these plugins, and what base class do I extend?
evals.expected_output: Use the Relationship plugin type: put the plugin in Plugin/Relationship/, annotate with @Relationship, and extend \Drupal\ctools\Plugin\RelationshipBase, implementing getRelationship().

evals.prompt: Which service ID manages CTools Relationship plugins, and how do I list the relationships available for a given set of contexts?
evals.expected_output: The plugin.manager.ctools.relationship service (RelationshipManager); call ->getDefinitionsForContexts($contexts) to list relationships available for those contexts.

evals.prompt: I need a per-user, expiring store for serializable objects in CTools, and a way to load one of those objects out of the shared tempstore as a route parameter. Name the relevant CTools services, and note any caveat.
evals.expected_output: ctools.serializable.tempstore.factory (SerializableTempstoreFactory) is the per-user expiring store, though it is deprecated in favor of core's shared tempstore factory; ctools.paramconverter.tempstore (TempstoreConverter) loads an object out of the shared tempstore for a route parameter.

evals.prompt: How do I wire up a CTools wizard route: what route key points at the wizard class, what route enhancer is needed, and what access requirement guards it with the tempstore lock check?
evals.expected_output: The route uses _wizard: My\Wizard\Class plus the ctools.wizard_enhancer route enhancer, and typically requirements: { _ctools_access: 'TRUE' } (backed by ctools.access / TempstoreAccess). Controllers ctools.wizard.form / ctools.wizard.entity.form render it.

evals.prompt: Which submodule ships with CTools adds a block that renders a single field of an entity? Name the submodule and briefly what the other two CTools submodules do.
evals.expected_output: ctools_block adds the entity-field block (render any single field of an entity). ctools_entity_mask lets a custom entity type borrow another type's fields/display, and ctools_views exposes a Views display as a configurable block with row/offset overrides.

evals.prompt: In CTools, which services do I use to turn stored context config into Context objects, and to resolve a typed-data property path (like node:uid:entity) to a context? Name them.
evals.expected_output: ctools.context_mapper (ContextMapper) turns stored context config into Context objects; ctools.typed_data.resolver (TypedDataResolver) resolves a typed-data property path to a context via getContextFromProperty().
